name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Surprise! Governor Scott lied
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31db/9e00690/439dc579/24039a01/2334386472/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&source=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG")
Hello --

For spending so much time in Florida, Donald Trump doesn't seem too worried about ruining the Sunshine State.

Last month, Trump's Administration announced plans to auction off drilling rights for 90% of our nation's ocean acreage, making the Pacific, Atlantic, and Gulf coasts, as well as the Arctic, available for development.

This is a direct attack on coastal states like ours -- but we have a chance to stop it.

**[The Department of the Interior has recently opened a public comment period, so add your name to demand they protect our waters.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31db/9e00690/439dc579/24039a00/2334386472/VEsE/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/no-coastal-drilling/gillum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2+-+The+Department+of+the+Interior+has+recen&utm_campaign=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&source=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG")**

Our coastlines are among the most majestic places on Earth -- yet Trump's administration is turning over these areas to the world's biggest polluters. This will not only devastate marine life and threaten our beaches, but jeopardize the economies of coastal communities.

And while Governor Rick Scott promised to protect Florida's coastlines and stand up to Trump's administration, he ultimately went back on his promise to further his own political ambitions -- while Floridians pay the price.

**[We can not let Trump and Scott sell our environment for short-term profits. Add your name now and make your voice heard.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31db/9e00690/439dc579/24039a03/2334386472/VEsF/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/no-coastal-drilling/gillum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=3+-+We+can+not+let+Trump+and+Scott+sell+our+&utm_campaign=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&source=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG")**

Thank you,

Andrew Gillum

**"It is disappointing that despite support from a bipartisan majority of U.S. Senators, this bill was blocked from further consideration... I urge the Senate to reconsider its decision and pass legislation that will celebrate, cherish, and protect life."** **-President Donald J. Trump** Loyal Supporter, When he addressed the March for Life in January, President Trump called on the U.S. Senate to pass a bill banning painful late-term abortions after 5 months of pregnancy. Last week, the Senate voted on the bill. _46 Senators heartlessly voted against the bill, preventing it from passing the filibuster threshold of 60 votes._ **_Loyal Supporter, do you agree with President Trump**_ that we must celebrate, cherish, and protect life? Please add your name and stand with President Trump to express your disgust at the heartless actions of these U.S. Senators - and to show that you will keep fighting! [](http://trk.listelixr.net/c/4/?T=OTkyNjA2OTc%3AMDItYjE4MDM2LWNhMzhiN2MxZWE5ZjRiM2Q5Zjc5MjE4NTg0OWFlOGM2%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AMjgyNTQ5Mjg%3AZmFsc2U%3AMQ%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2JhbGlzdGFjdGlvbi5jb20vc3RhdGVtZW50LW9mLWRpc2FwcG9pbnRtZW50LTAyMDUxOGVjZC8&K=wIM-llwEHHWiKQp1JxD2aw) We both know these Senators cannot be allowed to get away with their cruel attack on innocent unborn children. That's why my organization, Susan B. Anthony List, has been on the ground in Florida, Ohio, Missouri and Indiana since July of last year. Our dedicated team of over 270 canvassers (and growing) are going door-to-door, educating voters on the pro-abortion records of vulnerable Democratic Senators who are up for re-election in November. **Any Senator who is so extreme that they would vote against this compassionate pro-life bill _must be immediately put on notice._** Add your name now to stand with President Trump against the Senators who are fighting to ensure unborn children are cruelly dismembered by painful late-term abortions. **[ADD MY NAME](http://trk.listelixr.net/c/4/?T=OTkyNjA2OTc%3AMDItYjE4MDM2LWNhMzhiN2MxZWE5ZjRiM2Q5Zjc5MjE4NTg0OWFlOGM2%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AMjgyNTQ5Mjg%3AZmFsc2U%3AMg%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2JhbGlzdGFjdGlvbi5jb20vc3RhdGVtZW50LW9mLWRpc2FwcG9pbnRtZW50LTAyMDUxOGVjZC8&K=I1ppCL-AB6FOSEh-eckiNw)** Loyal Supporter, if you're like me, you're sickened by the actions of these Senators. They are more devoted to abortion corporations like Planned Parenthood than they are to innocent unborn children whose Constitutional right to life is being trodden upon. I hope you'll stand with President Trump and our incredible canvassing team by adding your name today. For _LIFE_ , Marjorie Dannenfelser
President, Susan B. Anthony List P.S. After the vote last week, I instructed my field directors to tell our canvassers to share with voters about their Senators' betrayal of unborn babies. And I've asked our team to find room in our budget to expand into further states to ensure these senators' horrific actions do not go unanswered. **[ADD MY NAME](http://trk.listelixr.net/c/4/?T=OTkyNjA2OTc%3AMDItYjE4MDM2LWNhMzhiN2MxZWE5ZjRiM2Q5Zjc5MjE4NTg0OWFlOGM2%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AMjgyNTQ5Mjg%3AZmFsc2U%3AMw%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2JhbGlzdGFjdGlvbi5jb20vc3RhdGVtZW50LW9mLWRpc2FwcG9pbnRtZW50LTAyMDUxOGVjZC8&K=TYUyRedjtFCFfycNKiPEhw)** | 1200 New Hampshire Ave. NW, Suite 750, Washington, DC 20036

name: The White House
subject: What tax cuts mean for Ohio
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c4c6f2815314ce1cd973ba15a216aa9b37e7780b205dd10d797e82a932d8a1ef75fd14bca8324b2df4c96a8e7a63a79a9e8c37d2f43ad19

What tax cuts mean for Ohio

This afternoon, President Donald J. Trump visits Cincinnati, Ohio, for the second time during his Administration. He’ll be joined by Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin and Republican legislators from Ohio to discuss what tax cuts and reform has meant for the state.

The President will tell Ohioans that since he signed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act in late December, more than 300 companies have delivered bonuses, raises, or 401(k) investments to more than 3 million American workers. The result of the bill has been historic tax relief for Americans not seen since President Ronald Reagan’s Administration.

Before he speaks to Ohioans, President Trump will participate in a tour of the Sheffer Corporation—a manufacturer of hydraulic, pneumatic, and custom cylinders—and meet with Ohio business leaders.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c4c6f2815314ce1f554e7934abd1ceb2512105e152b4504f83d14812a5ab2938547c6d002f47055a0d3b7200e20489fca521b81e6929635
Watch the President’s remarks live at 2:30 p.m. ET.

The President meets with North Korean defectors

President Trump met with eight defectors from North Korea Friday in the Oval Office. One person from the group, Ji Seong-ho, had joined the President as a special guest at last Tuesday’s State of the Union Address.

“[Seong-ho] really made an incredible impression on me and on everybody else, both on television and in that magnificent room,” the President said. “I had an opportunity to meet with some of the folks, and their stories are amazing.”

The defectors shared personal stories of their harrowing escapes from North Korea, as well as details on their experiences living under Pyongyang’s repressive Kim regime. The group of escapees met with other senior U.S. officials during their trip to Washington.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c4c6f2815314ce1db4767eb025b8c928d74f7ee4fc488c589a126199e0d8246224f28ee88a39f69153b89623dc6dab641e45bc2fd50eea4
Watch as President Trump meets with these heroic North Korean defectors.

Jobs gains top off a banner week

Friday morning’s jobs report showed 200,000 new jobs in January, capping off a week of stellar economic news that included rising wages, higher confidence, and expanding manufacturing.

The headlines tell the story:

- BLOOMBERG: U.S. Added 200,000 Jobs in January; Wages Rise Most Since 2009
- CNBC: Pay Gains During Trump’s First Year in Office Best Since the Great Recession
- MARKETWATCH: Wage Increases Reported by Highest Number of Companies in 18 Years, Survey Finds
- WASHINGTON EXAMINER: U.S. Oil Production Hits 10 Million Barrels per Day for First Time Since 1970

Read more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c4c6f2815314ce1e21cdc59c056956830a1e4f8f47e50b80113158a129d6bac1163c446c75eb89e37cbedd95663c1a41c64e1990d53f178
Jobs numbers top off an impressive week of economic news

Bonus read:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c4c6f2815314ce137d209a9d73eac612f1978380b0f2ffd4bbd246a087feb024ffa09a958489f7be37f71270e6ed70b16875ed6c0581032
Council of Economic Advisers shares good news on wage growth

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump watch a performance by the Florida Atlantic University marching band and cheerleaders prior to Super Bowl LII | February 4, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

This morning President Trump met with Vice President Mike Pence.

Later, the First Lady will accompany the President to Ohio to participate in a briefing on opioids and a patient meet and greet at Cincinnati Children’s Hospital Medical Center.

In the afternoon, The Vice President and Second Lady will depart Washington, DC, en route to Anchorage, Alaska, to participate in a United States Northern Command briefing with aerospace and missile defense leadership.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c4c6f2815314ce16eeba9d33a345a4ce08b0c44ada3763c78191ba0838c0773dc2e4d1af1e3690d3103ec7453bf6cf1a1c306a9086f00b1

Contact the White House |

name: Tim Kaine
subject: One of the most emotional moments of my political career
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek,

There are many tough days in the Senate -- days where progress seems so far away. We need reminders that we have the power to do great good. I recently experienced one such reminder, and I'd like to tell you about it -- this is a long email, but it's important to me, and I hope you'll read it:

In January, we obtained a major victory for Virginia as Congress finally passed -- after decades of effort -- our bill giving federal recognition to six Virginia Indian tribes. These tribes -- the Chickahominy, Eastern Chickahominy, Upper Mattaponi, Nansemond, Monacan and Rappahannock -- were among the original tribes populating Virginia when English settlers arrived at Jamestown in 1607. They entered into peace treaties with the English in the 1670s and have maintained tribal identity and traditions since that time.

But they were long treated with contempt and discrimination by Virginia -- their records destroyed, their students denied access to in-state higher education opportunities, their artifacts and even the bones of their ancestors taken by museums. And even as more than 500 tribes across the country have been recognized by the federal government, these living and thriving tribes had not.

Through it all, our tribes have carried themselves as sovereign people peacefully living within a nation they love. Their commitment to military service has been especially notable. And each year, the tribes journey to Richmond the day before Thanksgiving to celebrate their connection to Virginia by presenting a tribute to the Governor. How Anne and I used to relish that celebration during our days in the Governor's Mansion! And yet how ashamed it made me feel to travel with the tribes to England and see them recognized and celebrated there as a sovereign people by reason of their peace treaties when America -- their land and home -- refused to acknowledge them.

Beginning in the 1980s, there has been a slow turning to justice for our tribes. Virginia officially recognized the tribes and then our elected officials -- both Democrats and Republicans -- joined with them to push for federal recognition. The first bill in Congress was introduced by Congressman Jim Moran in 2000 and the process has been painfully slow. One Virginia tribe, the Pamunkey, received recognition through an administrative process in 2016. But the six tribes lacked records to meet the administrative standard. Courthouse fires during the civil war and active destruction of their records by state officials blocked their path. They had to depend upon Congress.

Over the years, the recognition bill passed the House twice without Senate action. More recently, the Senate would act favorably in committee, but the recognition bill would die on the Senate floor. But finally, on January 11th, the stars aligned. Senator Warner and I were able to clear the last Senate objection -- essentially getting to unanimity within the Senate. With tribal leadership in attendance, we called up Congressman Rob Wittman's previously passed House bill, passed it by voice vote, and sent the matter on to President Trump for signature. It was one of the most emotional moments of my political career.

In the Senate Hall after the vote, I told the Chiefs that I was so proud of them for never giving up. I have learned so much from them along the way.

When a Virginia Senate seat unexpectedly opened in 2011 after Jim Webb (a great champion of the tribes) decided not to run for re-election, I was not sure whether I should run. But one question I asked myself was whether, after 16 years of public life, there was any unfinished business that I still wanted to complete. And the issue of our tribes still being unrecognized came to mind as one of just a handful of things that I still hoped to accomplish.

Even in a tough time, moments of grace occur and important work gets done. That prospect -- the next good thing I can do -- keeps me going every day.

Thanks for standing with me.

Tim

name: Carlos Adolfo Gonzalez via Democrats.org
subject: [Add your name] Dream Act now
body:
The time for Congress to act is now. Join me today to demand a Dream Act immediately.
---
I'm an American, Derek -- just not on paper.

After my father passed away from cancer when I was a small boy, my mother brought me and my two sisters from the Dominican Republic to the United States to build a better life here.

For the last 16 years, I've lived in Lancaster, Pennsylvania. It's my home -- the only one I've really ever known. Yet under the current system, there's no way for young undocumented immigrants like me to come out of the shadows and gain citizenship.

The Dream Act would do just that -- it would create a path for undocumented youth to stay here in the U.S. and fully contribute to society without fear of deportation.

Dreamers can't wait any longer, and it's up to us to keep the pressure on Congress to pass this legislation immediately. **Add your name to tell Congress to pass the Dream Act now:**

[ADD YOUR NAME](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/pass-DREAM-Act-now?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20180205_cg_nd&source=em_20180205_cg_nd")
Today, there are nearly 800,000 Dreamers who are living in fear that we will have to leave our families and our homes. Thousands of people are already losing their protected status and could face deportation.

To the members of Congress contemplating whether or not to support the Dream Act with a path to citizenship, I'd say this: Put yourself in our shoes -- consider how long we have been here. Our most formative years have been spent in this country and it is ours to call home.

The time for Congress to act is now.

**[Add your name to tell Congress to pass the Dream Act and create a pathway to citizenship for Dreamers like me.](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/pass-DREAM-Act-now?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+tell+Congress+to+pass+t&utm_campaign=em_20180205_cg_nd&source=em_20180205_cg_nd")**

I'm not going to stop believing in or fighting for this country. I hope you don't either.

Carlos

If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052210b/4067b0a/549ebea1a/7e5250b8/3623148873/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180205_cg_nd&source=em_20180205_cg_nd"). Thanks for supporting our party.
clean_body: The time for Congress to act is now. Join me today to demand a Dream Act immediately. I'm an American, Derek -- just not on paper. After my father passed away from cancer when I was a small boy, my mother brought me and my two sisters from the Dominican Republic to the United States to build a better life here. For the last 16 years, I've lived in Lancaster, Pennsylvania. It's my home -- the only one I've really ever known. Yet under the current system, there's no way for young undocumented immigrants like me to come out of the shadows and gain citizenship. The Dream Act would do just that -- it would create a path for undocumented youth to stay here in the U.S. and fully contribute to society without fear of deportation. Dreamers can't wait any longer, and it's up to us to keep the pressure on Congress to pass this legislation immediately. Add your name to tell Congress to pass the Dream Act now: "" Today, there are nearly 800,000 Dreamers who are living in fear that we will have to leave our families and our homes. Thousands of people are already losing their protected status and could face deportation. To the members of Congress contemplating whether or not to support the Dream Act with a path to citizenship, I'd say this: Put yourself in our shoes -- consider how long we have been here. Our most formative years have been spent in this country and it is ours to call home. The time for Congress to act is now. "" I'm not going to stop believing in or fighting for this country. I hope you don't either. Carlos Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Hello,

First off, thank you for joining the team.

Here at Team Gillum, we know it's possible to have a major impact on a campaign and on an election -- even if you aren't a billionaire or ultra well-connected lobbyist. We know that the only way we can truly rebuild Florida into a state that works for everyone is if _everyone_ is included in the process.

After witnessing Doug Jones' historic win in the Alabama U.S. Senate special election, it's more clear than ever. Black voters, especially Black women, played a major role in his victory. When people of all different races, ethnicities, and backgrounds come together, they will win.

**[And that's exactly what we're doing here at Team Gillum. If you're with us, follow Andrew on Facebook to join the fight.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31da/9e00690/43eef829/24039a90/170097074/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/andrewgillumfl/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2+-+And+thats+exactly+what+were+doing+here+a&utm_campaign=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG&source=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG")**

It's going to take a lot of power, hope, and determination to rebuild Florida after Governor Rick Scott's eight years in office. But after the momentum we've seen since launching this campaign, we're more confident than ever that we can do it. Together.

It's critical that everyone exercise their right to vote and make their voice heard, even when some try so hard to stop you. Everyone, no matter your background, can make a difference and impact -- and Andrew will need you on Election Day.

**[When people work together, anything is possible. So join Team Gillum and stay up-to-date with the campaign by liking us on Facebook now.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31da/9e00690/43eef829/24039a93/170097074/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/andrewgillumfl/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=3+-+When+people+work+together+anything+is+po&utm_campaign=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG&source=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG")**

Did you see this? Trump is using "religious freedom" as an excuse to legalize discrimination by your doctor.

[**Help us fight back -- sign on to demand an immediate repeal of this new rule >>>**](http://act.floridadems. "http://act.floridadems.org/page/s/condemn-trump-s-attacks-on-women-s-healthcare?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=1+-+Help+us+fight+back++sign+on+to+demand+an&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2")

**Begin forwarded message:** **Alert: Trump Administration to allow healthcare providers to discriminate against women
[SIGN ON: Condemn Trump's latest attack on women's healthcare >>>](http://act.floridadems. "http://act.floridadems.org/page/s/condemn-trump-s-attacks-on-women-s-healthcare?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+SIGN+ON+Condemn+Trumprsquos+latest+attac&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2")** Friend,

Did you miss the Trump Administration's new rule undoing one of President Obama's protections for women's health?

**Doctors in all 50 states are now allowed to turn away patients based on moral or religious grounds, opening the door for a wave of discrimination, particularly against women.**

This is a thinly-veiled attack on women, women's access to birth control, and women's access to legal abortion.

A woman's right to access the healthcare she needs should never be subject to the personal or religious beliefs of her healthcare provider.

[**SIGN ON: Demand that doctors be barred from discriminating against patients on religious or moral grounds >>>**](http://act.floridadems. "http://act.floridadems.org/page/s/condemn-trump-s-attacks-on-women-s-healthcare?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=3+-+SIGN+ON+Demand+that+doctors+be+barred+fr&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2")

This move comes on the heels of Senate Republicans' attempt to pass a 20-week abortion ban, and is underscored by the fact that Trump made zero mentions of women's health or women's rights in his State of the Union Address.

But now Trump is coming between patients and life-saving health procedures in order to push his extremely conservative agenda.

[**Join us in demanding that this rule be scrapped immediately >>>**](http://act.floridadems. "http://act.floridadems.org/page/s/condemn-trump-s-attacks-on-women-s-healthcare?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=4+-+Join+us+in+demanding+that+this+rule+be+s&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180D_180205_1_2")

Thanks for standing with us,

Florida Dems

body:

Peter,

Next week, we can prove that Democrats -- and more specifically pro-choice Democratic women -- are going to keep racking up wins in 2018.

Three of our endorsed women are running in special elections for state legislative seats in Minnesota and Florida on February 12th and 13th.

As they enter the home stretch of their campaigns, it's critical to show that this community is invested in winning down ballot races.

**[Donate $3 now to help elect our women next week and all of our state and local candidates running this year.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c552f/24c69fd9/1222d7062/1894e0f1/27764877/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180205_email_DaysToSpecial?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+now+to+help+elect+our+women+nex&utm_campaign=WQEFEN06N_digital_email_20180205_DaysToSpecial_inact&source=WQEFEN06N_digital_email_20180205_DaysToSpecial_inact")**

Our candidates' historic wins over the last year have shown that when we invest in pro-choice Democratic women running in local races -- even in districts Trump won -- we have the opportunity to remake the halls of power.

In Minnesota and Florida, Melissa Wagner and Margaret Good are running to turn state House seats blue. And Karla Bigham's race for the Minnesota state Senate may affect which party controls that chamber.

If they win, it means more elected officials who protect Planned Parenthood and a woman's right to choose, advocate for paid family leave, and fight for equal pay.

And electing these women is critical to showing that pro-choice Democratic women can win in all kinds of districts.

**Donate $3 now to support Karla, Melissa, Margaret, and all of our women running in 2018:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Elections-Next-Week](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c552f/24c69fd9/1222d7062/1894e0f2/27764877/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180205_email_DaysToSpecial?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgElectionsNextWee&utm_campaign=WQEFEN06N_digital_email_20180205_DaysToSpecial_inact&source=WQEFEN06N_digital_email_20180205_DaysToSpecial_inact")**

Thanks,

Lucinda Guinn

body:
Earlier today, I officially filed paperwork to serve my community in Indiana’s 6th Congressional District. I got in this race because... | | |
---|---|---
**I’m Ready to Serve, Again!**

Earlier today, I officially filed paperwork to serve my community in Indiana’s 6th Congressional District.

I got in this race because I was encouraged to step up after Congressman Luke Messer announced his candidacy for the Senate. But before I would even consider such an important role for my community, I reached out to Republican leaders across the Sixth District to better understand what type of Congressman they want to represent this important area of our state.

_I took the time to meet with each of them to make sure I knew what matters most to my fellow Hoosiers as I embark on this journey to serve my community once again._

The Sixth District has been my home for nearly 60 years. I believe **President Trump’s America First agenda** will benefit Hoosiers, especially us here in the Sixth District.

I will fight alongside the President to advance the Trump-Pence agenda to grow the economy, protect our borders, and provide for our fighting men and women - both during and after their service.

[**Become a part of our team by joining Team Pence now >>>**](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=0b0bf04be61b490da8d41925c443007c&_e=3GUOVJj98HtBU5A1F8Qeo42dzEbGhujjs085WNMjMSVReRRqZCXDJI4wXG6oR6lemNhqd4iy-aLPA64MKTkT_QZEq9xowna0bIYJmM3yDH5aMK6VpkU4iWxyeNdV5S8aVnCOXZB9mRTMsBDgUVUudlEq5boR-JvNTTuoYvTPejfYrS-EHN8E3H86bqdhvcGPJq8qpMBX_hVqX7O4LF1kwx1MB7fNaI1qaZFZ_CV_VX-CPm5QxE56fULo5mG8V3Vc-ZhzVpTaDACtbJcmND9acf0ulE_adMzMbNcod_8kP6cL5KZs9CyXCF0_5H7maxEm80pX3GWD0pEYXZb13QBcxKSC4-x5AOQfd4BwYGGmcVOYXahWEzRQC5mC2Qi0hMlP0RKjXZu-KZgR0dBYOEvSqMmbtEV7DAlyO4vZ48Bf3sQ%3D)
